questions.question: Define the term 'one-to-one' in the context of functions. Provide an example to illustrate this.
questions.hint: Consider a simple function, such as f(x) = 2x, and explain how it is or is not one-to-one.

questions.question: A function f: A -> B is said to be one-to-one if every element in B is mapped to by at most one element in A. Explain why this property is important.
questions.hint: Focus on the concept of uniqueness and how it relates to function notation.

questions.question: Describe the difference between a plain function and a one-to-one function. Provide an example of each.
questions.hint: Consider simple examples, such as f(x) = x^2 for a plain function and f(x) = 2x for a one-to-one function.
